key_insight: PA-SciML adds a verification-first layer to agentic SciML so candidate surrogates are judged by explicit physics checks, not just low prediction error.
engineering_relevance: For solid mechanics surrogate modeling, this helps ensure reported models respect boundary conditions, stiffness scaling, superposition, and causality before they are trusted in simulation workflows.
method: Fix the scoring evaluator before search.
Derive machine-checkable physics requirements and audit each candidate’s outputs.
Search input/load-history ranges for high-violation cases without needing reference fields.
evidence_signal: In the static elasticity example, the selected surrogate achieved lower validation error than an error-only baseline while passing the shared linear-elastic checks; in the transient case, a similar-error baseline failed a causality test but PA-SciML’s selected model passed.
watch_item: Physics verification is only as strong as the chosen checks, so incomplete or poorly specified requirements could still miss important failure modes.
paper_title: Physics-Audited Agentic Discovery in Scientific Machine Learning
